title: Royal Mail Wants Part-Time Workers to Help Deliver More Letters
summary: Royal Mail, the UK postal service, is asking part-time postal workers to work more hours to help deliver letters on time. They have been criticized by the government and a regulator for not meeting delivery targets. For example, they are only delivering 75% of first-class letters on time, but the target is 93%. Royal Mail plans to invest £500 million over five years to improve things, like giving workers familiar routes and managing sickness. They will also deliver some lower-priority mail less often, but parcels will still come daily. The company says it needs to update its old rules for delivering mail. Workers have complained about hiding mail to meet targets, but the union supports the new plan if workers vote for it.

a group that helps workers have a say in their jobs

Why is Royal Mail being criticized?
options: For not delivering letters on time
questions.correct_answer: For not delivering letters on time

How much money will Royal Mail invest over five years?
What will happen to low-priority mail?
Who needs to vote for the new plan to be approved?

background_read: Royal Mail is a big company in the UK that sends letters and parcels to people's homes. They have rules called the Universal Service Obligation that say they must deliver letters six days a week to every address. Recently, they have been struggling to meet their goals for on-time delivery, which has made the government and a watchdog called Ofcom unhappy. To fix this, they are making changes like asking workers to work more and spending money on improvements. Workers have shared stories about hiding mail to make it look like they are doing better, but the union is hopeful the new plan will help.
Article_Structure: WHO: Royal Mail, part-time postal workers, the government, Ofcom (regulator), Communication Workers' Union
WHAT: Royal Mail is asking part-time workers to work more hours and investing £500 million to improve letter delivery and meet targets
WHY: Because Royal Mail is not delivering enough letters on time and has been criticized, so they need to make changes to fix the service

perspectives.description: Royal Mail believes that by investing money and having workers do more hours, they can improve delivery times and meet their targets within a year.
Some postal workers have complained about being asked to hide mail to meet targets, but the union supports the plan if it gives workers better tools and reverses chaos.
